snippets.title: Minimal src-layout project tree
snippets.code:
ragapp/                           # repo root
├── pyproject.toml
├── README.md
├── src/
│   └── ragapp/                   # the importable package
│       ├── __init__.py
│       ├── chunker.py
│       └── retrieval/
│           ├── __init__.py
│           └── faiss_backend.py
├── tests/
│   └── test_chunker.py
└── .github/workflows/ci.yml
snippets.explanation: `src/ragapp/` is the only place importable code lives. Tests, CI config, README — everything else — sits *outside* `src/`.

snippets.title: pyproject.toml — telling the build backend about src/
snippets.code:
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=64", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = "ragapp"
version = "0.1.0"
requires-python = ">=3.10"
dependencies = [
  "numpy",
]

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
where = ["src"]            # <-- the key line

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["tests"]
pythonpath = []            # don't add the cwd; rely on installed package
snippets.explanation: `where = ['src']` makes setuptools auto-discover `src/ragapp/` and ship it as `ragapp` in the wheel. `pythonpath = []` keeps pytest honest: it won't fall back to importing from the source tree.

snippets.title: Hatch backend variant
snippets.explanation: Same idea, different backend: `packages = ['src/ragapp']` tells hatch where to find the package in the source tree.

snippets.title: Develop loop with src layout
snippets.code:
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate

# editable install — code changes take effect immediately,
# but ragapp is properly on sys.path via installed metadata
pip install -e .

# tests run against the installed package, not the source tree
pytest -q

# verify import goes through the install path
python -c 'import ragapp, sys; print(sys.modules["ragapp"].__file__)'
snippets.explanation: Editable install (`pip install -e .`) gives you fast iteration without weakening the layout — the import still goes through the installed dist.

snippets.title: Common mistake: leaving a stray top-level package
snippets.code:
ragapp/                  # ❌ this is also at repo root
├── src/
│   └── ragapp/
│       └── chunker.py
└── ragapp/              # ← shadows src/ragapp/ when cwd is on sys.path!
    └── chunker.py
snippets.explanation: If you copy a package to `src/` but leave the old one at the root, `import ragapp` may pick up either depending on `sys.path` order. Delete the old top-level copy.
